data.title: Ionis gets FDA approval for Tryngolza
data.description:
The FDA has approved Ionis Pharmaceuticals' (NASDAQ:IONS) drug Tryngolza for the treatment of familial chylomicronemia syndrome, or FCS.

The product, also known as olezarsen, was approved as an adjunct to diet to reduce triglycerides in adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome, according to the FDA’s approved drugs database.

The FDA had accepted the drug’s market application in June, with priority review and a target action date of Dec. 19.

data.title: FCPT acquires a P.F. Chang's property for $4.8M
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), on Thursday, announced the acquisition of a P.F. Chang's property for $4.8 million.
  • P.F. Chang’s China Bistro is an American-based, casual dining restaurant chain with over 200 locations across the United States, serving Asian fusion cuisine.
  • The property is located in a highly trafficked, strong retail corridor in Illinois and under a corporate triple net lease with approximately 5 years of term remaining.
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.4% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.
  • Four Corners Property Trust (FCPT) also announced the acquisition of a MercyOne outpatient clinic property for $2.8 million.
  • The property is located in a strong retail corridor in Iowa and is under a triple net lease with approximately five years of term remaining.
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.8% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.

data.title: Nike in charts: Footwear sales fall 11% while apparel sales inch lower in FQ2
Hydro One expands transmission ownership in northern Ontario
data.description:
• Hydro One (TSX:H:CA) said on Thursday that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Hydro One Networks, has entered into an agreement to purchase an approximately 48 percent interest in the East-West Tie Limited Partnership from affiliates of OMERS Infrastructure Management and Enbridge Transmission Holdings.
  • The East-West Tie Limited Partnership owns the East-West Tie Line, a 450-kilometre, 230 kV double-circuit transmission line, regulated by the Ontario Energy Board, spanning from Wawa to Thunder Bay, along the north shore of Lake Superior.
  • Hydro One has agreed to purchase its interest in the partnership for $257 million in cash, subject to customary adjustments.
  • The transaction results in a partnership with the remaining owners of the East-West Tie Line – the Bamkushwada Limited Partnership, a consortium of six First Nations, and affiliates of NextEra Energy Canada, who own approximately 3.5 percent and 48 percent respectively.

data.title: Greenway Greenhouse Cannabis acquires Choice Growers' CPG brands
data.description:
• Greenway Greenhouse Cannabis Corporation (OTCQB:GWAYF) said on Thursday that it has entered into an asset purchase agreement with Choice Growers Cannabis.
  • Under the purchase agreement, Greenway has acquired all of Choice Growers' consumer packaged goods brands, SKUs and listings of the brands, and other associated intellectual property in exchange for (i) the indebtedness owed by Choice Growers to Greenway, and (ii) a royalty payment equal to varying percentages of net revenue over a period of six years.
  • This acquisition encompasses all of Choice Growers' brands, including Grapefruit God Bud, The Jeffrey, Watermelon Pebbles, Pink Lemonade, etc.

data.title: Tharimmune files to sell 1.44 million shares of common stock by selling shareholders
data.description:
• Tharimmune (NASDAQ:THAR) filed a prospectus related to the offer and resale of up to about 1.44 million shares of common stock.
  • This prospectus is not an offer to sell.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of ordinary shares by the selling shareholder.
  • Filing

data.title: Victory Capital announces $200M share buyback program
data.description:
• Victory Capital Holdings (NASDAQ:VCTR) board approved a new common stock repurchase program authorizing the repurchase of up to $200M of its common stock through December 31, 2026.
  • The company’s prior $100M stock repurchase authorization, approved in Q4, 2023, has been completed.
  • VCTR shares were up 1% in after hours trading on Thursday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Victory Capital to buy back up to $200M of its stock
data.description:
Victory Capital Holdings (NASDAQ:VCTR) said Thursday its board has approved a new share buyback program authorizing the repurchase of up to $200M of its common stock through Dec. 31, 2026.

“This new repurchase program is the largest in our history and allows us to remain flexible and opportunistic with our capital,” said Victory Capital Chairman CEO David Brown.

The Company’s prior $100M stock buyback authorization, approved in the Q4 2023, has been completed.

(NASDAQ:VCTR) shares gained 1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Prestige Wealth regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Prestige Wealth (NASDAQ:PWM) on Thursday said it has received a Nasdaq notice, informing that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement of $1.00 per share for continued listing.
  • PWM +33.83% after hours to $1.78.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: US Steel falls after Q4 EBITDA guidance cut
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) on Thursday fell over 5% in extended trading after the company lowered guidance for its fourth quarter EBITDA, saying that demand and pricing in Europe remains weak.

The company expects fourth quarter adjusted EBITDA to be about $150 million, down from the previous forecast of between $225 million and $275 million. It also sees fourth quarter adjusted net earnings per diluted share of ($0.29) to ($0.25).

“Steel prices remained depressed and BR2 ramp-related costs exert pressure on the quarter, while the Big River team works towards increasing prime ton production in our new mill,” said CEO David Burritt, adding that the company’s Flat-Rolled segment’s adjusted EBITDA is expected to be lower than the third quarter due to lower selling prices and volumes, and increased outage and maintenance activity.

The steelmaker said that it also expects the Mini Mill and the European segment’s adjusted EBITDA to be lower than in the third quarter due to lower volumes.

The company’s stock was trading at $30.16 in trading after the bell on Thursday. Overall, it has lost nearly 35% so far this year.
